teachingPoint: Point calculation is always server-side. T84 reads scorePercent from questionnaireResult (stored record), derives points via a FREEDOM formula, and never reads earnedPoints from the incoming request payload. Client-submitted earnedPoints enable point farming: any client can inflate their score.
positiveExample: T84 reads questionnaireResult.scorePercent from DB, applies FREEDOM formula → pointBreakdown{}.
negativeExample: T84 reads input.earnedPoints and stores it directly — client controls the ledger.
discriminatingConstraint: earnedPoints field does not exist in T84 input shape. The field is absent from the contract, not zero-checked.
keywords: Point calculation server-side reads scorePercent questionnaireResult derives points FREEDOM formula earnedPoints incoming

teachingPoint: Streak calculations must use local date in the learner's timezone, not UTC midnight. A learner at UTC-8 who completes a lesson at 23:00 local time (07:00 UTC next day) must be credited to the local calendar day. UTC-based streak breaks their chain at exactly the worst moment — late-night completion.
positiveExample: T96 receives userTimezoneOffset, computes localDate = utcNow + offset, compares to last streak date.
negativeExample: T96 uses new Date().toISOString().slice(0,10) — UTC midnight boundary, breaks streaks for non-UTC learners.
discriminatingConstraint: userTimezoneOffset is a required input field. Absence = contract violation. UTC = silent correctness failure.
keywords: Streak calculations local date learner timezone midnight UTC-8 completes lesson time next

teachingPoint: Privacy-gated branches require a double gate: T90 checks privacySetting at entry (Branch C gate), and T91 triggers on SocialShareApproved (output of T90), not on QuestionnaireAnswered. Triggering T91 directly on QuestionnaireAnswered bypasses privacy consent and distributes content for private-mode users.
positiveExample: T90 reads privacySetting → private: return success({shared:false}). T91 entry: @EventPattern('SocialShareApproved').
negativeExample: T91 triggers on QuestionnaireAnswered — fires for all users including private-mode. No consent gate.
discriminatingConstraint: T91 event pattern is SocialShareApproved — never QuestionnaireAnswered. The gate is structural, not conditional.
keywords: Privacy-gated branches require double gate checks privacySetting entry Branch triggers SocialShareApproved output

teachingPoint: ML adaptation output must pass three guards before being applied: (1) count ceiling (max N modules changed per adaptation — FREEDOM key), (2) protected modules check (certain modules are MACHINE-locked from ML modification), (3) recency window (last adaptation < 7 days → skip). Unbounded ML changes destabilize curriculum structure silently.
positiveExample: T89 applies guards in order: count ≤ maxChanges, no protected modules, lastAdaptedAt < now-7d → apply.
negativeExample: T89 applies all ML recommendations directly — no ceiling, no protection, no cooldown. Silent curriculum corruption.
discriminatingConstraint: All three guards are mandatory. Missing any one = CF-05-ML violation. Guards run before any storeDocument call.
keywords: adaptation output pass three guards being applied count ceiling modules changed FREEDOM

teachingPoint: LearningFlowCompleted gates on Branch A completion only — not Branch B (ML) or Branch C (social). B and C are time-decoupled async branches. Waiting for ML adaptation (2-5s) or social distribution (10-30s) before emitting LearningFlowCompleted violates the <1s UX SLA for the gamification reward display.
positiveExample: T98 triggers on GamificationBatchStored (T85 output) — Branch A only. B and C complete independently.
negativeExample: T98 waits for MLAdaptationCompleted and SocialDistributionCompleted — user waits up to 30s for completion badge.
discriminatingConstraint: LearningFlowCompleted is a Branch A output. Branches B and C have no wire to T98.
keywords: LearningFlowCompleted gates Branch completion social time-decoupled async branches Waiting adaptation distribution emitting

teachingPoint: Achievement unlock requires a history read before emitting AchievementUnlocked. T87 checks xiigen-achievements for an existing record with (userId, achievementId) before writing. Without this check, concurrent GamificationBatchStored events each trigger T87, both find no record, both write, both emit — double unlock with duplicate badge notification.
positiveExample: T87: searchDocuments({userId, achievementId}) → count > 0 → return success({alreadyUnlocked:true}). Else storeDocument → emit.
negativeExample: T87: no history read — storeDocument always, emit AchievementUnlocked always. Concurrent calls = double unlock.
discriminatingConstraint: History read is a precondition gate, not an optimization. Missing read = silent double-emit.
keywords: Achievement unlock history read emitting AchievementUnlocked checks xiigen-achievements existing userId achievementId writing

teachingPoint: Gamification point calculation is a server-side operation. The input shape does not include earnedPoints. The server reads the stored questionnaire result, applies a FREEDOM formula, and produces pointBreakdown{}. Any pattern where the client submits earnedPoints creates a point farming vector and is a CF-05-1 violation.
keywords: Gamification point calculation server-side operation input shape include earnedPoints server reads questionnaire
ironRules: T84 input schema MUST NOT include earnedPoints field — field is structurally absent, not zero-checked
Points ALWAYS calculated server-side from stored questionnaire result (scorePercent)
FREEDOM config provides the scoring formula — never hardcoded constants

teachingPoint: Streak date boundaries must use the learner's local timezone. UTC midnight does not correspond to the end of the learner's day. userTimezoneOffset is a required contract field. The local date is computed as utcTimestamp + offsetMinutes, then formatted as YYYY-MM-DD for streak comparison. CF-05-2 enforces this.
keywords: Streak date boundaries learner local timezone midnight correspond userTimezoneOffset required contract computed
ironRules: userTimezoneOffset is a REQUIRED input field — its absence is a contract violation
Streak boundary = midnight in user local timezone: localMs = utcMs + (offsetMinutes * 60000)
UTC midnight comparison silently breaks streaks for all positive-offset timezone users
local date = new Date(localMs).toISOString().slice(0, 10) in YYYY-MM-DD format

teachingPoint: Privacy-gated async branches must use structural gating: the branch entry node checks privacy and emits a consent event (SocialShareApproved) only when sharing is permitted. Downstream nodes in the branch listen on the consent event — not on the original trigger. This prevents private-mode content from entering the social distribution pipeline regardless of conditional logic in downstream nodes.
keywords: Privacy-gated async branches structural gating branch entry checks privacy consent SocialShareApproved sharing
ironRules: T90 checks privacySetting at branch C entry — private users return DataProcessResult.success({ shared: false })
Gate is structural (event pattern change) not conditional logic in T91
Private-mode users never reach T91 regardless of conditional checks inside T91

teachingPoint: ML-generated recommendations must pass three mandatory guards before application: (1) count ceiling (max N changes — FREEDOM), (2) protected module check (certain modules are MACHINE-locked), (3) recency cooldown (skip if last adaptation was within threshold). All three guards run before any storeDocument call. Missing any guard = silent curriculum corruption.
keywords: ML-generated recommendations pass three mandatory guards application count ceiling changes FREEDOM protected
ironRules: GUARD 1: count ceiling — max N module changes per adaptation (FREEDOM key: ML_ADAPTATION_MAX_CHANGES)
GUARD 2: protected module check — MACHINE-locked modules removed from suggestions before application
GUARD 3: recency cooldown — skip if lastAdaptedAt < threshold (FREEDOM key: ML_ADAPTATION_COOLDOWN_DAYS)
All three guards run BEFORE any storeDocument call — missing any one = silent curriculum corruption
